title: capsella nudicaulis
intro: Capsella nudicaulis, also known as shepherd's purse, is a biennial plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight infections. The health benefits of shepherd's purse include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, shepherd's purse is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and can also be used fresh or dried in cooking and medicine.
